mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW X6 xDrive35d / 40d engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned X6 diesel engine typically costs £3,500–£9,500 supply only depending on generation and model. A replacement engine for an X6 can cost between £4,500–£6,000, with dealer estimates often in the £14,000–£18,000 range. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,800 at independent specialist rates. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW X6 xDrive35d / 40d engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on which generation you're asking about. The M57 (E71, 2008–2010) is robust but injector and turbo issues are common. The N57 (E71/F16, 2010–2019) is prone to timing chain stretch, piezo injector failure, turbo oil starvation, and connecting rod bearing issues. The B57 (G06, 2019+) is generally regarded as a reliable unit, but timing chain and DPF/EGR issues have been reported. Across all, oil-change discipline is everything.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW X6 xDrive35d / 40d?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain stretch and failure (N57 and B57), piezo injector failure (N57), turbocharger oil starvation and failure (N57), DPF clogging and EGR system issues (B57), connecting rod bearing wear (N57), and ZF 8HP transmission issues. See the Era Map above for generation-specific advice.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW X6 xDrive35d / 40d engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value. Against a well-kept E71 X6 (values £5,000–£12,000+), a £4,000–£5,500 recon is 30–70% of the car's value — borderline but often worth it for a good example. Against a £10,000–£20,000 F16, a £4,500–£6,000 recon is 25–50% — often worth it. Against a £20,000–£40,000 G06, a £5,500–£6,500 recon is 15–25% — worth it. The BMW scrap threshold of 70% means evaluating the whole vehicle and its condition before committing.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW X6 xDrive35d / 40d engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. As a rule of thumb: E71 xDrive35d (2008–2010) = M57D30; E71/F16 xDrive35d (2010–2014) = N57D30; F16 xDrive40d (2014–2019) = N57D30B; G06 xDrive40d (2019–2022) = B57D30B (340 bhp); G06 LCI xDrive40d (2022–present) = B57D30B (352 bhp with MHEV). Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the N57 timing chain, or does it require engine replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A preventative timing chain, tensioner and guide replacement costs £1,500–£3,500 — one owner reported paying £1,800 for the work. However, if the chain snaps, a full engine replacement (£4,000–£7,500) is usually required. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately.

eraMap.title: The X6 xDrive35d / 40d THROUGH TIME — Era Map

rows.eraNote: Early M57 — 286 bhp, 580 Nm; robust but injector, turbo and timing chain issues are well-documented
N57 — 306 bhp, 600 Nm; timing chain stretch, piezo injector failure and turbo oil starvation are key watchpoints
Revised N57 — 313 bhp, 630 Nm; improved but injector failure can destroy engines
B57 twin-turbo — 340 bhp, 700 Nm; refined and powerful but timing chain and DPF/EGR issues need monitoring
Latest B57 with 48V mild hybrid — 352 bhp, 700 Nm; proven B57 engine with added MHEV complexity

eraMap.sourceNote: This table is the single source every other section on this page matches — Sections 2, 4 and 6 reference it rather than restating conflicting figures.

replacementCosts.h2: BMW X6 xDrive35d / 40d Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the current X6 xDrive40d range. Older generations: see the Era Map above for E71/M57, E71/F16/N57 and F16/G06/B57-specific pricing. BMW engine replacement costs typically range from £2,500 to over £10,000 depending on the engine model. A replacement engine for an X6 can cost between £4,500–£6,000, with dealer estimates often in the £14,000–£18,000 range.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an X6 engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £75–£110/hr (independent) or £130–£180/hr (London/SE dealer). Labour costs for BMW engine replacements in the UK typically range from £800 to £3,000. [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet]

cta.label: → Compare X6 xDrive35d / 40d engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Timing Chain Stretch & Failure (M57, N57, B57)
problems.symptoms: Rattling or clattering noise from the engine, especially during startup; engine warning light; loss of power; metallic noises; rough idling; potential engine failure if chain snaps.
problems.typicalMileage: 80,000–120,000 miles (N57) — one owner reported timing chain replacement at 152,000 miles
problems.repairCost: £1,500–£3,500 (timing chain, tensioner and guide replacement) — one owner paid £1,800 for timing chain work
problems.replacementCost: £4,000–£7,500 (if chain snaps and valves meet pistons)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: The N57 engine is prone to excessive timing chain wear which can lead to premature failure. Symptoms can include a rattle on start-up or a change in engine note where the chain is hitting on the guides — sometimes they give you no warning at all. A cold-start rattle is the early warning sign — ignore it and you risk piston-valve contact and a full engine replacement. Regular oil changes with the correct BMW-approved oil are the best prevention. At the first sign of rattle, get it inspected immediately. One X6 owner reported having to do the timing chain immediately after purchase at 152,000 miles.

problems.title: Piezo Injector Failure (N57 — E71, F16)
problems.symptoms: Rough idling, engine misfires, loss of power, decreased fuel efficiency, check engine light, smoke from exhaust, "Drivetrain Malfunction" warning.

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you experience rough idling or loss of power, book an inspection immediately

problems.recommendation: Failure of piezo injectors is a well-documented issue on N57 engines. One X6 xDrive40d owner reported a catastrophic failure where the second cylinder injector failed, destroying the engine — covered by warranty at a cost of approximately £18,000. If you notice rough idling, misfires, or loss of power, have the injectors inspected immediately. Replacing a faulty injector before it fails completely can prevent engine destruction. Regular fuel filter changes and using high-quality diesel are the best prevention.

problems.title: Turbocharger Oil Starvation & Failure (N57)
problems.symptoms: Loss of power, whistling or whining noise from the engine, excessive exhaust smoke, reduced fuel economy, engine warning light.
problems.replacementCost: £4,000–£7,500 (if turbo failure sends debris into the engine)【PLACEHOLDER】

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear whistling or experience power loss, book an inspection immediately

problems.recommendation: Oil starvation of the turbine is a common problem on the N57 engine. The powerful diesel engines in the X6 can show turbocharger problems, particularly if oil changes have been neglected. Turbocharger failures can lead to a loss of power and increased exhaust smoke. If you notice a whistling sound or loss of power, have the boost system inspected immediately. Regular oil changes with the correct BMW-approved oil and allowing the engine to cool after hard driving are the best prevention.

problems.title: DPF & EGR System Issues (B57 — G06)
problems.symptoms: DPF warning light, reduced power, increased fuel consumption, frequent regeneration cycles, engine warning light, rough idle, "underboost code".
problems.typicalMileage: 40,000–80,000 miles (urban-driven cars are at higher risk)
problems.repairCost: £200–£800 (DPF clean, forced regeneration, or sensor replacement)【PLACEHOLDER】

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon; can lead to costly emissions system damage

problems.recommendation: DPF clogging is a common issue on diesel X6 models, particularly on cars driven mostly in the city. One owner reported a DPF blockage causing an underboost code and the DPF being blocked, requiring a long drive to attempt regeneration. The EGR system can also fail, with the bypass flap bolts on the EGR module potentially loosening due to manufacturing defects. If you see a DPF warning light, take the car for a sustained motorway run at 2,000–2,500 rpm. If the light persists, have the DPF inspected. Regular motorway driving and using premium diesel can help keep the DPF healthy.

problems.title: Connecting Rod Bearing & Crankshaft Issues (N57)
problems.symptoms: Knocking noise from the bottom of the engine, especially under load, low oil pressure warning, metallic debris in oil filter, eventual engine seizure.

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear knocking, stop driving immediately

problems.recommendation: Cranking of the connecting rod bushings is a known problem on the N57 engine. If you hear a knocking noise from the bottom end, stop driving immediately and have it inspected. Regular oil changes with BMW-approved oil are the key prevention — many specialists recommend 8,000-mile intervals.

problems.title: ZF 8HP Transmission Issues (All Generations)
problems.symptoms: Harsh or delayed shifting, jerky gear changes, torque converter shudder, transmission fluid leaks, failure to engage gear.
problems.repairCost: £500–£2,000 (fluid change, mechatronic repair, or solenoid replacement)【PLACEHOLDER】

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon if shifting issues develop

problems.recommendation: The ZF 8HP transmission is widely used across BMW's range and is generally robust. However, common issues include worn clutches, defective solenoid valves in the transmission control unit and contaminated transmission fluid. Similar problems occur in the 30d and 40d diesel variants, but usually only after 100,000–150,000 kilometres. BMW's "lifetime fill" claim is widely disputed — many specialists recommend transmission fluid changes every 60,000–80,000 miles.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: BMW X6 xDrive35d / 40d — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Catch it early — far cheaper than engine replacement
Replacement is straightforward; catch it before engine destruction
Viable if caught before debris enters engine
Cleaning or forced regeneration is cost-effective
Recall work may be available; out-of-warranty repair is still viable
Viable if caught early; often requires full rebuild
Piston-valve contact means little is left to repair

buyingChecks.title: Buying This X6 xDrive35d / 40d? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with oil changes at 8,000–10,000 mile intervals (not BMW's extended CBS schedule)
* E71 (2008–2014): injectors replaced or functioning correctly and no turbo noise
* F16 (2014–2019): timing chain inspected and no cold-start rattle
* G06 (2019+): no DPF or EGR warning lights and regular motorway use
* Timing chain work already completed (especially on higher-mileage cars)
avoidIf: * No proof of regular oil changes — these engines are unforgiving of neglect
* Cold-start rattle present — timing chain work is imminent and expensive
* Any injector or turbo warning lights — potentially catastrophic failure ahead
* DPF or EGR warning lights — emissions system repairs can be costly
* Any engine warning light without diagnosis — these engines can hide expensive faults

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The BMW X6 xDrive35d and xDrive40d represent the performance diesel flagships of BMW's Sports Activity Coupé range. The E71's M57 and N57 engines offer proven performance but demand injector, turbo and timing chain vigilance. The F16's N57D30B is a solid performer but injector failure can be catastrophic. The G06's B57 is a significant step forward in performance and efficiency, with mild-hybrid technology on later cars, but DPF, EGR and timing chain issues need attention. Across all generations, one rule applies: oil-change discipline is everything. BMW's extended service intervals are widely considered inadequate — treat your X6 diesel to 8,000–10,000 mile oil changes and it will reward you with hundreds of thousands of miles of effortless motoring. One owner's experience sums it up: "A wonderful car to drive, much loved" but requiring vigilance — a replacement engine in February 2020 cost around £18,000 under warranty.

cta.label: → Compare X6 xDrive35d / 40d engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW X6 xDrive35d or xDrive40d (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattle from the engine (timing chain warning — critical on N57 and B57)
* Service History — Including which engine era (M57/N57/B57) and confirmed oil-change discipline (8k–10k miles max)
* Injector Health — Any rough idle or misfires? (N57 injector failure warning)
* Turbo Health — Any whistling, power loss, or smoke? (turbo oil starvation warning)
* DPF Health — Any warning lights? Ask about regeneration frequency; urban cars will have more issues
* EGR System — Any warning lights or loss of power? Check recall status
* Transmission — Any harsh shifts or hesitation? ZF 8HP issues may be developing
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Timing, injector, turbo, DPF, or EGR codes are most common

However, if the chain snaps, a full engine replacement (£4,000–£7,500) is usually required. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately.
